assertions.description: The analyzer correctly handled multi-unit BAV70W components (8 units = 16 pins but 8 physical packages). Component type breakdown matches source file contents.
Correctly identifies separate decoupling domains for +5V and +3V3 rails. Cap values and counts match source.

Row driver has Q1 (2N3904 NPN for column presence sense) and Q2 (FQP47P06 P-channel MOSFET for power switching from 24V). Both are present in the schematic but transistor_circuits array is empty. Q2 is a high-current PMOS power switch — a significant circuit element.

U7 is a Wurth WE-FDSM isolated 5V DC/DC converter module, which is a power regulator. power_regulators is empty. The component has 'DC/DC' in its name/value. This is a missed detection for a power conversion stage.

U13 is a TXB0108 8-channel bidirectional level translator between 3.3V signals (from WeMos/Arduino) and 5V logic for the TPIC6B595 shift registers. Signals like RC_DATA_3v3, RC_SHIFT_CLK_3v3 vs RC_SHIFT_CLK show the voltage domain crossing. This is an important architectural feature (mixed 3.3V/5V system) that is undetected. The net classification does identify _3v3 suffixed signals but does not connect them to the level-shift context.
